trail.val: Q3 $1.9B; YTD $5.3B; raised FY26 to ~$9B (4.5x FY25)
P200 design wins NOT in the $9B; now selling G300 (102.4T) with no win/order yet

forward.note: Core forward driver; FY26 order target raised 3x through the year, but mgmt itself calls AI orders nonlinear/lumpy.

trail.val: ~$4B AI revenue FY26; expect >=$6B hyperscaler AI revenue FY27

forward.note: FY27 >=$6B AI-rev frame to be confirmed at the Q4 print (first formal FY27 guide).

trail.val: new Cat9k smart switches; multiyear cycle starts
record campus orders +25%, wireless +40% (WiFi7 half mix)
Cat 4k EOL concludes end-CY26, Cat 6k end-CY27 (6k far larger base = 2027 bigger wave); ~$40B aged gear in scope
~12-18 month cycle, CSCO gaining share at Juniper's expense; revenue outgrowing units on price

forward.note: EOL is the structural forcing function; 2027 is the bigger leg given the larger 6k install base.

trail.val: 67.5% NG GM; memory cost + price increases
66.0% NG GM (-260bps YoY); mgmt says 'stabilized'; Q4 guide midpoint also ~66%
GM flat Q3->Q4 despite AI mix; op margin the more controllable lever as hyperscale scales

forward.note: AI optics ~40% GM vs ~68% aggregate (JPM) is the structural mix drag as AI hardware scales vs flat software.

trail.val: >$1B orders in Q3; on track >200% YoY FY26
650 Group 'very bullish' on 1.6T PAM4 receiver - first inside-DC organic optic, millions of units, multibillion-$ opportunity

forward.note: Optics is the bull's second leg; Acacia capped near-term by assembly/CPO ramp.

trail.val: $10B+ incremental DCI TAM; $300-500M content per DCI connection; Arista+Cisco best-positioned for switch
first two Silicon One P200 scale-across design wins (plus a third early Q4); 'at scale' only in FY27

forward.note: P200 scale-across maps to JPM's DCI TAM but is not yet in the $9B AI-orders number.
